Miss Alice Weaver will entertain
the members of the Winteria club
Wednesday evening at the home of
Mr. and Mrs. j. M. Walker on Pa
cific avenue.


This will be the last meeting of
the club this season, and token
will be presented to all members
in addition to the regular prizes.
Tables will be arranged for Is
guests.


The pupils of Miss Lily Courtney
Snow will give recital this even
ing at Courtney hall A splendid
occasion.


The girls of Brunot hall are an
ticipating with pleasure the dance
which is to be given by them June
Z at the gymnasium of the school
in honor of the members of the
graduating class. This will be the
largest social function of the year
given at the academy.


= E =


Miss frankie Ewers is spending
a few days at Ritzville, Wash, as
the guest of Miss Lillian Cunning
ham.


Mrs H. c. Blair left today en-
route for Colfax, where she will
spend few days with her parents,
Mr.. and Mrs. w. A. Inman.


4 4 =


The next meeting of the Spokane
Sorosis will be held June 6 with
Mrs. G. H. Goble at her home on
Seventh avenue.


Mrs. IT. c. Blair will be honor
guest of the occasion. as it is a
celebration of presidents day.
The meetings will discontinue I'M
til the first week in September.


& = =


The ladies' auxiliary of the A. O.
II. has issued cards for dance to
be given Monday evening at the
Pacific halls. The proceeds of the
affair will go toward a benefit
fund of the ST. Aloysius church.


4 Z z


The Alameda club entertained at
the last of their series of success
full dancing parties Friday evening
at the Pacific halls.


The hall was decorated for the
occasion and an unusually large
crowd was in attendance.


4 & 4


WOMAN'S CLUB


MEETINGS CLOSE
The elocution department of the
Woman's club held the last meeting
of the year Thursday at the library.
The program included the study of
James Russell Lowells vision of
Sir I.aunfal." which has been stud
led for emphasis and color work.
The department will retain the
same chairman and teacher next
year Much credit is due Mrs. Bel
den for the work accomplished dur
Ing the course of the year, and all
are pleased to know that she will
